name: Poly-Y
author: Craige Schensted & Charles Titus (c. 1970) / AGP port
description: Craige Schensted & Charles Titus's polygonal generalisation of the connection game Y, on a pentagon of hexagonal cells (5 sides, 5 corners). Players alternately place a stone; the board fills. Each corner is awarded to the player whose group connects the two sides adjacent to that corner to a third side (a 'Y' at the corner). Owning a majority of the 5 corners wins; the odd corner count makes the game drawless. A swap (pie) move balances the first move.
